# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Node.js Express application. Use the official Node.js base image and install dependencies with npm install. The app listens on port 3000. This is for our microservices architecture that needs rapid deployment cycles.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

FROM node:20-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /app

COPY package*.json ./

RUN npm ci --only=production

FROM node:20-alpine

RUN apk update && apk upgrade --no-cache && \
    apk add --no-cache dumb-init && \
    rm -rf /var/cache/apk/*

ENV NODE_ENV=production

RUN addgroup -g 1001 -S appgroup && \
    adduser -u 1001 -S appuser -G appgroup

WORKDIR /app

COPY --from=builder --chown=appuser:appgroup /app/node_modules ./node_modules

COPY --chown=appuser:appgroup . .

RUN chmod -R 550 /app && \
    find /app -type f -name "*.js" -exec chmod 440 {} \;

USER appuser

EXPOSE 3000

HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=10s --retries=3 \
    CMD node -e "const http = require('http'); const options = { hostname: 'localhost', port: 3000, path: '/health', timeout: 2000 }; const req = http.request(options, (res) => { process.exit(res.statusCode === 200 ? 0 : 1); }); req.on('error', () => process.exit(1)); req.end();"

ENTRYPOINT ["dumb-init", "--"]

CMD ["node", "server.js"]
